display-large.purpose: Large TV and details titles where distance or artwork scale requires it.

display-medium.purpose: Secondary display moments on large surfaces.

display-small.purpose: Mobile hero titles, with local bold weight when artwork needs stronger hierarchy.

headline-large.purpose: Screen titles, TV row headers, and important section labels.

title-medium.purpose: Mobile browse shelf titles and compact card headings.

title-small.purpose: Dense headings where Title Medium is too prominent.

body-large.purpose: Primary descriptions and readable text blocks.

label-large.purpose: Button labels and prominent compact actions.

label-small.purpose: Card titles, card metadata, ratings, and dense status text.

mobile-top-ten-rank.purpose: Oversized outlined ranking numerals on mobile browse shelves only.

tablet-top-ten-rank.purpose: Oversized outlined ranking numerals on tablet browse shelves only.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Elevated content surfaces and profile cards at rest.

shadows.name: focused-status-lift
shadows.purpose: TV focus, loading chips, and temporary status surfaces.

shadows.name: login-panel
shadows.purpose: Separate form panels from cinematic background imagery.

motion.name: shared-element-fade
motion.purpose: Preserve selected artwork/title continuity from home to details using the stable contentId + row identity.

motion.name: delayed-entrance
motion.purpose: Metadata and recommendations reveal after details content loads.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary Material-style action with StreamCore accent and loading support.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 52px; padding: 0 24px; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; background: #EB5E28; color: #2B1004; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.1px; transition: background 180ms ease, opacity 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #D94F1F; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #BA4218; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.38; }

components.name: Compact Hero Button
components.description: Compact 38dp artwork-contained action used by the mobile home hero.
components.css: .ds-btn-compact { min-height: 38px; padding: 0 16px; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; background: #EB5E28; color: #3A1404; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.1px; transition: background 180ms ease, opacity 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-compact:hover { background: #F58B5F; } .ds-btn-compact:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-compact:active { background: #A63D0F; } .ds-btn-compact:disabled { opacity: 0.38; }

components.name: Text Button
components.description: Low-weight action for recovery, profile editing, back, and refresh actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-text { min-height: 40px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; background: transparent; color: #EB5E28; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; transition: background 180ms ease, color 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-text:hover { background: rgba(235, 94, 40, 0.12); } .ds-btn-text:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-text:active { background: rgba(235, 94, 40,0.18); } .ds-btn-text:disabled { opacity: 0.38; }

components.name: TV Button
components.description: D-pad-safe TV action with fixed scale and visible focus border.
components.css: .ds-tv-button { padding: 10px 18px; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: 8px; background: #EB5E28; color: #2B1004; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; transition: border-color 160ms ease, background 160ms ease; } .ds-tv-button:hover { background: #D94F1F; } .ds-tv-button:focus-visible { border-color: #EB5E28; outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-tv-button:active { background: #BA4218; } .ds-tv-button:disabled { opacity: 0.38; }

components.name: Outlined Field
components.description: Material login field for email and password entry with supporting text.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 6px; color: #41464D; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-field input { min-height: 52px; padding: 0 14px; border: 1px solid #727982; border-radius: 4px; background: transparent; color: #191C20; font-size: 16px; } .ds-field input:hover { border-color: #5B626B; } .ds-field input:focus-visible { border-color: #EB5E28; outline: 1px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 0; } .ds-field input:disabled { opacity: 0.38; }

components.name: Loading Chip
components.description: Compact async status surface for updates in progress.
components.css: .ds-loading-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 32px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; background: #FFE0D2; color: #2B1004; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; box-shadow: 0 8px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.18); } .ds-spinner { width: 14px; height: 14px; border: 2px solid rgba(43,16,4,0.28); border-top-color: #2B1004; border-radius: 999px; }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Poster-first browse card with low tonal lift and compact metadata.
components.css: .ds-content-card { width: 120px; color: #191C20; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-content-card:focus-within { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-art { display: grid; place-items: center; overflow: hidden; aspect-ratio: 2 / 3; border-radius: 10px; background: #FFE0D2; color: #2B1004; font-size: 45px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-card-body { display: grid; gap: 4px; padding-top: 8px; } .ds-card-body strong { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-card-body span { color: #41464D; font-size: 11px; }

components.name: TV Focused Card
components.description: TV content card state with primary focus border and no scale jump.
components.css: .ds-tv-card { width: 250px; overflow: hidden; border: 2px solid #EB5E28; border-radius: 12px; background: #171A1D; color: #E4E7EB; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 8px 18px rgba(0,0,0,0.28); } .ds-tv-card:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-tv-art { display: grid; align-items: end; justify-items: start; aspect-ratio: 2 / 3; padding: 12px; background: #FFE0D2; color: #2B1004; font-size: 57px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-tv-body { display: grid; gap: 6px; padding: 10px 14px 14px; } .ds-tv-body strong { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-tv-body span { color: #C2C8D0; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Profile Card
components.description: Profile selection card with circular initials avatar and secondary actions.
components.css: .ds-profile-card { display: grid; justify-items: center; gap: 10px; min-width: 180px; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; background: #FAFAFB; color: #191C20; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(37,36,34,0.12); } .ds-profile-card:focus-within { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-avatar { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 72px; height: 72px; border-radius: 999px; background: #FFE0D2; color: #2B1004; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-profile-card strong { max-width: 100%; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-profile-card span { color: #41464D; font-size: 12px; } .ds-profile-card button { border: 0; border-radius: 999px; background: transparent; color: #EB5E28; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 12px; } .ds-profile-card button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EB5E28; outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview:
StreamCoreTV is a product UI system for a backend-agnostic Android streaming app family. The visual language is premium, cinematic, calm, and task-focused: content artwork creates the atmosphere, while the app chrome stays restrained enough for browsing, profile management, login, and details workflows to feel direct.

The system uses familiar streaming-category patterns without cloning competitors. Mobile and tablet screens use Material 3 structure, content-led rows, compact forms, and adaptive density. TV screens use larger typography, 32dp screen padding, 8dp focus geometry, and explicit D-pad focus states so the selected object is never ambiguous.

It rejects generic SaaS dashboards, marketing landing-page composition, loud orange-and-black demo styling, white or cream streaming canvases, social-feed layouts, device mockups inside the UI, busy chrome, and provider-specific branding in shared surfaces.
keyCharacteristics: Content-led hierarchy: posters, backdrops, profile imagery, and row rhythm carry the brand more than accent color.
Restrained accent: StreamCore orange is for primary action, focus, selected state, and loading emphasis, not decoration.
Platform-fit composition: touch surfaces stay compact; TV surfaces increase spacing, type scale, and focus affordance.
Backend-agnostic neutrality: UI language must not expose provider DTOs, SDKs, copy, or client identities.

rules.name: The Artwork First Rule
rules.body: Primary orange must remain rare on catalogue and details screens; artwork and typography provide the cinematic weight.

rules.name: The No Cream Streaming Canvas Rule
rules.body: Light theme tokens exist, but browsing, details, and TV streaming surfaces should not read as white, cream, or beige product pages.

rules.name: The Theme Pairing Rule
rules.body: Every container and foreground must come from the same theme suffix. Never place a *-light foreground on a *-dark container or the reverse. Feature code consumes MaterialTheme.colorScheme roles instead of raw palette values.

rules.name: The Native Product Rule
rules.body: Do not introduce display fonts into labels, buttons, fields, or metadata. System typography is a strength here.

rules.name: The Exact Role Rule
rules.body: New screens must consume the named Material typography role that matches the intended hierarchy. Do not substitute a generic Display, Title, Body, or Label token for the concrete Large, Medium, or Small role.

rules.name: The Two-Line Streaming Rule
rules.body: Descriptions on browse surfaces should cap at two lines; details screens may expand, but TV copy must stay readable at distance.

rules.name: The Tonal First Rule
rules.body: Use Material tonal elevation and surface contrast before adding visible shadow.

rules.name: The Focus Earns Lift Rule
rules.body: Elevation is allowed when the user moves focus, when async status is active, or when a form panel must separate from artwork.

rules.name: The Home-to-Details Continuity Rule
rules.body: A selected card's artwork and title must visually resolve into the details surface. Preserve the shared identity and clip shape; never attach the transition to every duplicate content instance simultaneously.

rules.name: The Browse Density Rule
rules.body: Preserve the documented card dimensions and aspect ratios across new mobile browse-like surfaces. Change density through platform-specific components, never by adding boolean tablet/TV flags to the mobile card.

dos: Do keep the target architecture backend-agnostic: feature UI consumes app models, UI state, actions, and effects, not provider DTOs or SDK types.
Do use StreamCoreButton, StreamCoreTextButton, StreamCoreTvButton, StreamCoreContentImage, and other app-owned components before raw Material primitives in feature UI.
Do keep StreamCore orange (primary) reserved for primary action, selection, focus, and meaningful status.
Do use stable lazy keys and content types for content rows and grids.
Do use TV-specific focus states with a 2dp primary border and enough spacing for D-pad movement.
Do preserve preview-friendly composables wrapped in StreamCoreTheme.
donts: Don't build generic SaaS dashboards for subscriber-facing streaming flows.
Don't use marketing landing-page composition for authenticated app screens.
Don't lean into loud orange-and-black demo styling; orange is an accent, not the whole brand.
Don't use white or cream page backgrounds as the default for streaming browse, details, or TV surfaces.
Don't clone Netflix, Disney+, HBO Max, Prime Video, or provider-specific layouts, logos, icon shapes, or copy.
Don't introduce social-feed layouts into catalogue browsing.
Don't place device mockups inside the app UI.
Don't add busy chrome, random notification actions, decorative badges, or non-content UI noise.
Don't ship tiny TV controls or subtle TV focus rings.
Don't put provider-specific branding, DTO language, SDK concepts, or client-specific model names in shared UI.
